content: Biological pathogens (biological agents from Topic B) can be framed as naturally evolved intrinsic curiosity-driven autonomous agents that use an ICM-like mechanism to drive exploration of new host niches, similar to how 2025-configurable ICM modules drive swarm UAVs to search for novel targets. This perspective reframes viral host-jumping behavior as an intrinsic reward-driven reinforcement learning process rather than just random genetic mutation.
